content: p  iframe loading  lazy  src    color  23ff5500 auto_play false hide_related false show_comments true show_user true show_reposts false show_teaser true  width  100   height  166  frameborder  no  scrolling  no    iframe   p   div style  font-size  10px  color   cccccc  line-break  anywhere  word-break  normal  overflow  hidden  white-space  nowrap  text-overflow  ellipsis  font-family  Interstate,Lucida Grande,Lucida Sans Unicode,Lucida Sans,Garuda,Verdana,Tahoma,sans-serif  font-weight  100    a style  color   cccccc  text-decoration  none   title  The Robot Report Podcast  href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  The Robot Report Podcast  a     a style  color   cccccc  text-decoration  none   title  AI's Role in the Future of Robotics  Insights from 3Laws  href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  AI s Role in the Future of Robotics  Insights from 3Laws  a   div   p Episode 233 of  em The Robot Report  em  Podcast features Andrew Singletary, CEO, and Amir Sharif, chief operating officer of 3Laws. They discuss the evolution of the company s intelligent robot supervisor technology from academic research to practical applications, the challenges of startup leadership, and the importance of integrating safety into the design process.  p   p The conversation highlights the innovative approaches 3Laws is taking to democratize autonomy and ensure  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  safety  a  in robotics, emphasizing the need for deterministic systems in an increasingly AI-driven world. Any roboticist developing a new system should consider whether the Pasadena, Calif.-based  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  company  a  can help address safety-critical operations.  p   div class  margin-bottom margin-xsmall    p Singletary also explores the differences between  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  Supervisor Pro and Supervisor for ROS  a , and how these products cater to different market needs. He and Sharif explain the significance of customization, certification, and making safe robotics more accessible to smaller companies.  p    div   div id  attachment_587136  style  width  780px  class  wp-caption aligncenter   img loading  lazy  decoding  async  aria-describedby  caption-attachment-587136  class  size-full wp-image-587136  src    alt  headshots of Andrew Singletary, CEO, and Amir Sharif, COO of 3Laws.  width  770  height  385  srcset    770w,   300w,   150w,   768w,   368w  sizes  auto,  max-width  770px  100vw, 770px     p id  caption-attachment-587136  class  wp-caption-text  Andrew Singletary, CEO  l , and Amir Sharif, COO  r  of 3Laws.   Credit  3Laws  p   div   div class  margin-bottom margin-xsmall    p The discussion also mentioned these prior podcast episodes   p   ul   li class  entry-title   a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  Thomas Pilz on innovation and safety in robotics  a   li   li  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  3Laws on robot safety and the evolution of bipedal locomotion  a   li    ul    div   hr     h2 Show timeline  h2   ul   li 8 07   News of the week  li   li 24 45   Andrew Singletary and Amir Sharif, co-founders of 3Laws  li    ul   hr     h2 News of the week  h2   h3  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener   b Intrinsic is joining Google to advance physical AI in robotics  b   a   h3   p Just five years after spinning out from Alphabet Inc. s  moonshot factory,  Intrinsic Innovation is joining Google. The physical AI startup said that being part of Google will allow it to amplify its work across frontier AI, from R D through deployment and daily operations, enabling industrial use cases in manufacturing and logistics.  p   p The companies did not disclose financial details of the transaction. As a distinct group within Google, the company will continue evolving the Intrinsic platform, using the capabilities of Google Gemini models and Google Cloud, as well as working closely with Google DeepMind.  p   p Wendy Tan White, the current CEO of Intrinsic, will remain in her position. The rest of the unit s reporting and leadership team remains the same, an Intrinsic spokesperson told  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener   i The Robot Report  i   a  i .  i   p   h3  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener   b DoW shoots down Border Patrol drone operating in military space  b   a   h3   p The Department of War   a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  DoW  a   mistakenly shot down a U.S. Customs and Border Protection  CBP   a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  drone  a  in the Fort Hancock airspace about 50 miles southeast of El Paso, Texas. The engagement occurred within military airspace far from populated areas. The FAA confirmed there was no impact on commercial flights or civilian safety.  p   p Some reports characterized the incident as a significant escalation of the coordination failures reported earlier this month. While the previous airspace  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener  shutdown  a  was an administrative and public relations disaster, this latest event represents a direct  friendly fire  engagement between two federal agencies.  p   p The roles have shifted. In this case, the DoW acted as the aggressor, using its counter-unmanned aircraft system  C-UAS  authorities to neutralize the CBP s drone. This suggests a  double-sided  coordination gap where neither agency seems to know what the other is flying or firing.  p   blockquote class  twitter-tweet   p Read more   a href     a href     p   ABC News   ABC   a href    27, 2026  a   p   blockquote   p  script async src    charset  utf-8    script   p   h3  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener   b Wayve raises  1.2B with plans to bring robotaxis to London  b   a   h3   p Wayve Technologies closed a Series D round of  1.2 billion, bringing its post-money valuation to  8.6 billion. The London-based company said it plans to use the funding to accelerate commercialization of its  end-to-end AI platform  for autonomous driving.  p   p Founded in 2017, Wayve said its AI Driver does not rely on map-based systems and runs entirely on onboard vehicle compute. The company said its unified AI platform spans SAE Level 2  hands-off  operations through Level 4  eyes-off  driving across vehicles, brands, and markets.  p   p In the past year, Wayve claimed that it was  the first and only AV developer to drive zero-shot in more than 500 cities across Europe, North America, and Japan, meaning without city-specific fine-tuning before deployment.   p   h3  a href    target  _blank  rel  noopener   b VDMA warns Germany is losing ground in global robotics race  b   a   h3   p Germany s robotics and automation industry is preparing for another tough year. The VDMA Robotics   Automation Association, a German trade group, expects revenue to fall 5  in 2026 to about  14.1 billion   16.6 billion U.S. . That follows a 7  decline in 2025, marking the second straight year of contraction for one of the world s leading robotics clusters.  p   p Industry leaders said the problems go beyond a normal economic slowdown. Weak demand from key customers has hurt orders. At the same time, geopolitical tensions and broader economic uncertainty have made companies more cautious about investing in new automation equipment.  p   p According to the association, Germany s robotics industry is also losing ground internationally. Competitors, particularly in Asia, are expanding faster and gaining market share. The VDMA has warned that high costs, regulatory burdens, and slow decision-making in Europe are making it harder for local companies to compete.  p   hr     div style  text-align  center    a href     img loading  lazy  decoding  async  class  aligncenter size-full wp-image-568305  src    alt  SITE AD for the 2026 Robotics Summit save the date.  width  728  height  90      a   div   hr     p The post  a href    s role in the future of robotics  Insights from 3Laws  a  appeared first on  a href    Robot Report  a .  p
